title: Armed boat approaches bulk carrier 92nm southwest of Mukalla, Yemen
summary: UKMTO reports armed small boat with seven individuals approached bulk carrier in Gulf of Aden, raising maritime security threat levels in the region amid Houthi threats and resurging piracy.
contents:
## Armed Boat Approaches Bulk Carrier Off Yemen Coast

### Incident Details
The United Kingdom Maritime Trade Operations (UKMTO) reported a maritime security incident involving a bulk carrier approached by a small black-hulled vessel carrying seven armed individuals approximately 92 nautical miles southwest of Mukalla, Yemen on May 1, 2026.

### Location and Strategic Context
Mukalla sits on Yemen's southeastern coast in the Gulf of Aden, a critical maritime corridor through which a significant portion of global shipping transits between the Indian Ocean and the Red Sea. The incident occurred at the intersection of several heavily trafficked shipping lanes.

### Response and Advisory
The captain of the bulk carrier reported the approach to maritime authorities immediately. UKMTO advised all vessels in the region to exercise extreme caution and report any suspicious activity. Private maritime security firms recommended transiting at maximum safe speed, maintaining heightened bridge watch, and deploying anti-piracy measures including razor wire and water cannons.

### Broader Threat Environment
Yemen's Houthi movement has threatened to resume attacks on shipping through the Red Sea in response to the ongoing conflict involving Iran. Whether this incident connects to the Houthis or represents an independent piracy attempt remains under investigation. UKMTO raised threat levels around the Somalia coast to "substantial" this week following multiple hijackings.
